title: Quinn's Cozy Night
synopsis: Quinn feels scared at bedtime when he hears mysterious sounds outside his window, but with help from his loving family and his brave teddy bear, he discovers the sounds are just friendly night animals and learns to feel safe and cozy in his room.

pages.text: Quinn loved his soft blue pajamas and his cozy bed. But tonight, he felt scared. Strange sounds came from outside his window. "Whooo, whooo!" went something in the dark.

illustration.description: Quinn, a young Black boy with short curly hair, sits up in his bed wearing light blue pajamas with star patterns. His brown eyes look wide and worried as he clutches his striped blanket. His bedroom has warm yellow walls, a wooden bed frame, and a window showing darkness outside. The room is softly lit by a small nightlight.
illustration.alt: Quinn looking scared in his cozy bedroom at night

pages.text: Quinn pulled his blanket up to his chin. His heart beat fast. "Mama!" he called. "I hear scary sounds!" He felt very small in his big bed.

illustration.description: Quinn is now completely under his striped blanket with only his worried eyes peeking out. His teddy bear sits beside him on the bed. The room feels larger and more shadowy, with moonlight streaming through the window. His expression shows fear but also the comfort of being in his familiar room.
illustration.alt: Quinn hiding under his blanket with his teddy bear

pages.text: Mama came into Quinn's room. She had kind brown eyes and a gentle smile. "What sounds do you hear, sweetie?" she asked softly. Quinn felt a little bit braver with Mama there.

illustration.description: Mama, a Black woman with shoulder-length natural hair wearing a purple robe, sits on the edge of Quinn's bed. She has a warm, caring expression and is gently placing her hand on Quinn's arm. The room feels warmer and safer now with soft lighting from the hallway behind her.
illustration.alt: Quinn's mama sitting on his bed comforting him

pages.text: "Listen with me," Mama said. They heard "Whooo, whooo!" again. Then "Chirp, chirp, chirp!" Quinn held his teddy bear tight. "Those sounds seem big and scary," he whispered.

illustration.description: Quinn and his mama sit together on the bed, both tilting their heads to listen. Quinn clutches a brown teddy bear with a red bow tie. Outside the window, silhouettes of trees can be seen against the dark blue night sky. Both characters have concentrated, listening expressions.
illustration.alt: Quinn and mama listening to night sounds together

pages.text: Mama opened the curtains a little bit. "Look, Quinn!" In the tree sat a fluffy owl. "Whooo, whooo!" it said. Tiny crickets sang "Chirp, chirp!" in the grass. Quinn felt surprised and happy.

illustration.description: Quinn and his mama look out the window together. In the large oak tree outside, a friendly brown owl with big yellow eyes perches on a branch. Small crickets with smiling faces sit in the green grass below. The scene is illuminated by gentle moonlight, making everything look magical rather than scary.
illustration.alt: Quinn and mama looking at friendly night animals outside

pages.text: "They're just saying goodnight!" Mama laughed. Quinn giggled too. He felt brave now. "Goodnight, owl! Goodnight, crickets!" he called. His teddy bear felt brave too. Quinn snuggled down, feeling safe and cozy.

illustration.description: Quinn waves happily from his window while mama stands behind him with her arms around him. The owl waves back with its wing, and the crickets seem to be waving tiny legs. Quinn's face shows joy and confidence now. His teddy bear sits proudly on the windowsill. The whole scene radiates warmth and friendship.
illustration.alt: Quinn waving goodnight to his new animal friends

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Quinn feel when he first heard the sounds outside?
questions.explanation: Quinn felt scared when he heard the mysterious sounds from outside his window.

questions.question: Who helped Quinn feel braver?
questions.explanation: Mama came to Quinn's room and helped him listen to the sounds and discover what they were.

questions.question: What was making the 'whooo, whooo' sound?
questions.explanation: The 'whooo, whooo' sound was coming from a friendly owl in the tree outside.

questions.question: How did Quinn feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: After discovering the friendly night animals, Quinn felt brave and cozy in his bed.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.instructions: Help complete Quinn's story by choosing the right words!
pages.text: Quinn felt _____ when he heard sounds outside. His _____ came to help him listen. They found a friendly _____ in the tree!

pages.title: Feeling Words
pages.instructions: Choose the words that describe how Quinn felt!
pages.text: At first Quinn was _____, but then he felt _____ and _____ in his cozy bed.
